title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A two-family property at 65-67 Adamson St., Allston sold for $1,200,000, built in 1910 with 2,520 square feet.
A one-family contemporary home at 15 Somerset Drive, Andover, built in 1997, sold for $2,000,000 on a 46,348-square-foot lot.
A Boston condo at 682 Atlantic Ave., reaching 146,396 square feet, sold for $14,131,000, showcasing a high-end market presence.

title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye surprised a fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt during a Bruins game at TD Garden.
Maye and his wife attended the game, where he interacted with fans after appearing on the videoboard.
Earlier, Maye participated in a charity event in Quincy, raising over $4 million for Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers has been voted into the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2026.
Rivers led the Boston Celtics to their 2008 NBA Championship, their first since 1986.
Rivers, with a career coaching record of 1,191-861, is one of 11 coaches with over 1,000 wins.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye, Patriots quarterback, shaved his head for Granite Telecommunications' charity event, benefiting Boston Children's Hospital.
The 'Saving by Shaving' event raised $4.15 million, with donations matched by Granite CEO Rob Hale and his family.
Other participants included Joe Andruzzi, Greg Hill, Quincy Mayor Thomas Koch, and Boston Children's CEO Kevin Churchwell.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Officer Sean Cullen charged with improper storage of firearm after leaving loaded gun and taser unsecured in vehicle.
Charges followed a December domestic dispute at his West Roxbury home, leading to firearms being requested by responding officers.
Cullen's law enforcement certification suspended and he is placed on administrative leave pending legal proceedings.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: N.H. Gov. Kelly Ayotte and her security detail helped rescue a driver from a fiery crash at Bedford Toll Plaza.
A Massachusetts driver crashed into the New Hampshire toll booth on the Everett Turnpike, resulting in serious injuries.
Traffic was rerouted at Exit 13 as investigations continued, with partial reopening of northbound lanes by 5 p.m.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel addressed the Patriots' interest in acquiring Eagles receiver A.J. Brown, acknowledging a possible delay until after June 1.
Vrabel discussed quarterback Drake Maye, focusing on his development and leadership as New England prepares for the 2026 season.
The Patriots plan to prioritize best player available in the NFL Draft while considering roster areas like pass rush and tight ends.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, charged with murdering his wife, Diane Opper, 56, in Belchertown home after claiming his gun 'went off.'
Police responded to a 911 call; Diane Opper found with multiple gunshot wounds and declared dead at Baystate Wing Hospital.
Mark Opper, held without bail, faces a probable cause hearing on April 27 in Belchertown District Court.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, charged with 14 counts after allegedly shooting at police in Brockton.
The shootout followed a high-speed chase after police recognized Andrade's vehicle from a bulletin.
Andrade, hospitalized with a shoulder wound, is held without bail pending a May 1 hearing.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal crash occurred Tuesday on Route 20 in Charlton, involving two vehicles, officials said.
A speeding pickup truck driver, who refused to stop for police, collided with an SUV, killing its driver.
The crash is being investigated by Charlton police and Massachusetts State Police, with the DA's office informed.
